title: Fix Cross-Tenant Data Access Using Email as Identifier
description:
## Bug
Logging in with a new Salesforce user showed another user's data. The system was using email as the unique identifier, but the same email can exist across multiple Salesforce tenants (production, sandbox, etc.).

## Root Cause
Email addresses are not unique across Salesforce tenants. A user with the same email in a different org would match the existing record instead of creating a new one.

## Fix
Switched the unique identifier from email to `sfuid` (Salesforce User ID), which is globally unique across all Salesforce instances. This ensures proper user isolation per tenant.

## Validation
- New Salesforce PBO instance successfully created a new user record.
- Account record was created with the correct `SFOrgID` to capture the tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Users are uniquely identified by sfuid, not email
New users from different tenants create separate user records
Account records include the correct SFOrgID for tenant identification
Existing users are not affected by the change
middleware_changes: Changed user lookup/creation logic to use sfuid instead of email as the unique identifier.
data_model_changes: sfuid is now the unique identifier for users. Account records include SFOrgID for tenant identification.
implementation_notes: Critical security fix. Email is a VARCHAR attribute, not a key. The sfuid (Salesforce User ID) is globally unique and properly isolates users across tenants. Account records now capture the SFOrgID.
comment_summary: Reporter identified the root cause: email was being used as a unique ID but the same email can exist across tenants. Switched to sfuid. Validated that new PBO instances create proper user and account records.
